# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      
      # Cache node_modules
      - name: Cache node modules
        uses: actions/cache@v1
        with:
          path: node_modules
          key: ${{ runner.OS }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.OS }}-node-
      
      # Cache pip packages
      - name: Cache pip packages
        uses: actions/cache@v1
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.OS }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements*.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.OS }}-pip-
      
      # Cache any configuration files (replace with your own)
      - name: Cache config files
        uses: actions/cache@v1
        with:
          path: ./path_to_your_config_files
          key: ${{ runner.OS }}-config-${{ hashFiles('./path_to_your_config_files') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.OS }}-config-
      
      # Your build steps go here
